steps: Label the three consecutive integers. The first is \(x\), so the second is \(x+1\) and the third is \(x+2\).
Translate each phrase: "the sum of 4 and the second integer" = \(4 + (x+1)\); "the product of 17 and the third integer" = \(17(x+2)\); "is less than" = \(<\).

Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A: \(4+(x+1) > 17(x+2)\) — uses the correct expressions but the wrong direction (> instead of <). Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(4+(x+1) < 17(x+2)\) — correct expressions and correct inequality direction. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: \(4+(x+2) > 17(x+3)\) — uses the third and fourth integers instead of the second and third, and the wrong direction. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(4+(x+2) < 17(x+3)\) — uses the wrong integers (shifted by one). Eliminated.
